concept: Scatter / Broadcast / Spread Wide Like Seed
description: To scatter: to spread wide and outward — seed broadcast over a field, light across water, people across the earth. The wild sibling of 'thophe' (sow), which places seeds in prepared ground; 'thiwera' flings them to the wind and trusts the ground to answer. In Phi the word carries planting's hope, never violence: what is scattered is given somewhere to grow.
sound_symbolism: It opens on 'thi', the seed-syllable of 'thinoe', then spreads through 'we' to wide-open 'ra' — sowing audible inside scattering, which is the word's whole argument.
grammatical_notes: Verb: 'telo thinoe thiwera.' (The farmer scatters seed.) 'sileta keru thiwera.' (The sun scatters light.) From the Babel text: 'muila phea lo thinoe lo miona to thiwera.' (The earth scattered the peoples like seeds.) Contrast 'thophe' (sow), placement into prepared ground.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Broadcast is the sowing of abundance and of letting go: seed spent generously on ground you do not control. Regeneration begins with a scattering.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The oldest planting there is — the sower's arc across the field, seed flung by hand, the gesture every grain culture knows.
